workflow_runs.name: 🎨 Format Guard
workflow_runs.display_title: fix(jobs): post-756 review findings and ai review sticky restyle (#758)

head_commit.message:
fix(jobs): post-756 review findings and ai review sticky restyle (#758)

* docs: broaden adr-029 dash-tail risk note and log review fast-follows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scraping): dedupe split request keys before the count cap

clamp_split_request trimmed/blank-filtered/byte-capped other_keys but did not
de-duplicate before .take(MAX_OTHER_KEYS), so a repeated key wasted one of the
32 slots (the insert is idempotent anyway). De-dup first-seen order preserved
via a HashSet seen-check before the cap; self-pairs equal to the clamped
member_key are already dropped. Extended the clamp tests with a 33-entry,
2-duplicate case → 31 distinct pairs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jobs): guard agency list edits pre-load and cap the zod schema

Post-#756 AI-review-gate findings:

- AgencyCompaniesPreferences add()/remove() early-return when useJobPreferences
  is still undefined (pre-load) — otherwise a quick add builds the next list from
  an empty [] and replaces the user's saved agency list with just that one entry
  (single-column setter, but still column data loss). Mirrors the sibling-panel
  guards in 92e5302b, with a regression test asserting no mutate fires pre-load.
- JobPreferencesSchema.extraAgencyCompanies gains .max(500) to mirror the Rust
  MAX_EXTRA_AGENCY_COMPANIES cap (same pattern as otherKeys' .max(32)); IPC
  codegen unchanged (gen:ipc --check green).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(scraping): pin split-request dedup collapse and full cap capacity

The Stop review-gate asked for two explicit cases on clamp_split_request:
- duplicate other_keys collapse to ONE entry, first-seen order preserved;
- de-dup runs BEFORE the count cap, so repeats never steal slots — >32 distinct
  keys with one key hammered still yields the FULL 32 distinct keys, not fewer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: restyle ai review sticky comment into severity sections

Replace the cramped one-table sticky body with a claude-review-style
layout — bold verdict line, expanded Critical/High/Medium sections with
per-finding anchors, and a collapsed Low details block. Presentation
only: validateFindings/blockingFindings/exit-code logic (ADR-0008) is
unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

head_commit.message:
feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicate jobs with source chips and split (adr-029) (#756)

* feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicates with pair tombstones

Recompute-at-ingest cross-board job clustering per ADR-029. One pure module
(scraping/cluster) blocks by normalized company + title first-token, then joins
by cosine (cached same-space vectors) or trigram-Jaccard of normalized titles;
a user "not a duplicate" pair tombstone against any member vetoes the join.

Surfaces:
- scraping/cluster/{normalize,mod}.rs: fold/company/title normalization, agency
  flag, trigram-Jaccard, assign_clusters, new_cluster_count (+ heavy unit tests).
- vector.rs (L0): hoisted cosine, re-exported from commands::ai_provider so the
  L1 cluster module reuses it without an upward import (arch R7).
- dedup/mod.rs: DedupStore on dedup.db (pair tombstones, DataStore + Resettable).
- commands/scrape.rs::recluster_postings_cache wired after both scrape paths;
  postings::apply_cluster_annotations patches cache items by id.
- autopilot: FoundJob cluster fields; record_run clusters the merged list and
  returns the all-new-cluster count; cluster-aware minMatchScore retention.
- IPC: dedup_mark_not_duplicate (5-file flow, gen:ipc) + JobPreferences
  extraAgencyCompanies single-column setter; tauri-client + service hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scraping): clamp dedup split request at the command boundary

- dedup_mark_not_duplicate: server-side clamp (CWE-770) mirroring
  clamp_agency_list — cap other_keys at 32, byte-clamp member_key + each
  other_key (~200 B), drop blank/self keys; no-op when nothing usable. Never
  trusts the renderer's Zod bounds alone. Colocated tests cover the caps.
- scraping/cluster: normalize the agency extras once per assign_clusters call
  (is_agency_with) instead of re-normalizing the full list per posting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jobs): render cross-board clusters with source chips and agency filter

Renderer side of ADR-029 (Feature A). The backend already annotates postings
and autopilot found-jobs with clusterId/clusterCanonical/clusterMembers/isAgency;
this wires the UI to group, echo, and split them.

- Posting type gains the four optional cluster fields (matching the backend JSON).
- Shared ClusterSourceChips + AgencyChip in components/job (both features import
  them without crossing feature dirs); chips open member urls via useOpenExternal.
- Jobs list hides clusterCanonical===false rows (unannotated always shown) and
  adds a hideAgency session toggle next to the sort control.
- PostingRow / PostingListItem / JobDetailPane render source chips + agency chip;
  JobDetailPane adds an "All sources" section with a per-member "Not a duplicate"
  split calling useMarkNotDuplicate.
- AutopilotCard renders one row per cluster (counts track visible clusters) plus
  chips + agency + a split action scoped by autopilotId.
- Settings: AgencyCompaniesPreferences edits extraAgencyCompanies via the
  single-column setter.
- en/de i18n for cluster/agency/settings keys, TEST_IDS, and colocated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(scraping): add cluster acceptance fixtures for cross-board dedup

Close the ADR-029 acceptance gap: the same real-world job, parsed through two
real board parsers with two different wire shapes (germantechjobs XML feed +
Adzuna JSON), must collapse into one cluster with both source refs and the
canonical-preference winner. Fixture-driven, no network.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jobs): keep cluster chips non-interactive inside the split-view listbox

frontend-reviewer HIGH: focusable chip Buttons inside PostingListItem — a
role="option" row with tabIndex=-1 in the active-descendant listbox — broke the
APG pattern (option must have no focusable descendants), exploded tab stops, and
risked focus loss when the virtualizer unmounts a focused chip.

- ClusterSourceChips gains `interactive` (default true); when false it renders
  pure presentational badges (no Button, no click) so the option row stays a
  single tab stop. PostingListItem passes interactive={false}; PostingRow and
  JobDetailPane keep the interactive chips (the "open other source" action lives
  in the detail pane's "All sources" section).
- PostingListItem.test now renders the REAL chips and asserts the option row has
  zero focusable descendants + a chip click selects once (no double action).
- Harden SourceBadge: guard the PLATFORM_CONFIG lookup with Object.hasOwn so a
  hostile source (__proto__/constructor) falls through to the generic fallback
  instead of resolving an Object.prototype member and crashing on config.icon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(jobs): cover autopilot split action and dedup invalidation paths

testing-reviewer follow-ups (tests only):

- AutopilotCard: assert the cluster split fires markNotDuplicate with
  { memberKey, otherKeys, autopilotId } — the autopilotId is load-bearing (only
  this call site sends it, ADR-029 §h autopilot-record recompute). Forward
  data-testid through the test's Button stub so the split button is queryable.
- use-dedup: assert the split invalidates BOTH keys.postings.all and
  keys.autopilot.all on success (a dropped second invalidate would leave the
  AutopilotCard rows stale, undetected).
- ClusterSourceChips: a member with no board renders the hostOf(url) fallback
  label.
- Drop the unused `export` on the file-private ClusterMember interface (cleanup
  audit; every consumer declares its own inline shape).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(scraping): close cluster coverage gaps and share the posting cluster-input seam

Coverage (testing-reviewer):
- dedup command: multi-byte UTF-8 clamp test (€×150) with is_char_boundary
  assertion, guarding the char-boundary walk-back in clamp_bytes.
- DedupStore::import: malformed-row bundle → Err AND pre-existing rows survive
  (locks deserialize-all-before-mutate).
- cluster_aware_retain: mixed cluster [below-bar scored + unscored] → whole
  cluster dropped (ADR-029 §g).
- boundary pins: cosine at exactly 0.92 and trigram-Jaccard at exactly 0.90
  (54/60) both JOIN via the production `similar` `>=`, so a `>=`→`>` slip fails;
  plus a MAX_EXTRA_AGENCY_COMPANIES list-length cap test.

Refactor (folded in):
- Extract cluster::posting_cluster_input — the single JobPosting→ClusterInput
  seam; recluster_postings_cache now deserializes each cache item to JobPosting
  and calls it, and the aggregator acceptance test uses it too (no third
  hand-rolled mapping). Removed the now-unused str_field helper.
- Cleanup: drop the never-read ClusterAssignment.key field; delete the is_agency
  convenience wrapper (only test callers) — tests call is_agency_with directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: record cross-board clustering (adr-029) in the knowledge base

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: fix dedup store pointer and cluster-aware minmatchscore row

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jobs): surface dedup split failures instead of a false success toast

/review HIGH: dedup_mark_not_duplicate RESOLVES json!({"error": ...}) on
failure — Tauri never rejects a returned Value — so useMarkNotDuplicate never
threw and both split call sites showed the "Postings separated" success toast on
a silent failure.

- Widen the honest union end to end: DedupContract.markNotDuplicate and the
  tauri-client namespace now return { success: true } | { error: string }
  (mirrors AiContract.setActiveProvider).
- useMarkNotDuplicate narrows the resolved payload and throws on 'error' in
  result before the onSuccess invalidations run.
- JobDetailPane + AutopilotCard split call sites add onError → notify.error with
  a new jobs.cluster.splitFailed key (en + de; parity test enforces).
- Tests: use-dedup gains a case where the mock RESOLVES { error } and asserts
  the mutation rejects + does not invalidate; JobDetailPane asserts the error
  toast (not the success toast) fires on failure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: record full-row set() false-positive precedent in review config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: apply rustfmt to clustering sources

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: run export-render benchmark on main only with dispatch opt-in

pull_request events no longer trigger the 16-minute cargo bench; the
push-to-main baseline keeps the rust changes gate and workflow_dispatch
on any branch remains the per-pr opt-in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: point scraping-domain at cluster constants instead of restating values

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scraping): log tombstone read failures instead of silently re-merging

DedupStore::all_pairs swallowed a transient SQLite prepare/query error via
`.ok()`, returning an empty set — which silently re-merges previously-split
clusters on the next ingest with zero trace. Keep the degrade-to-no-splits
policy but `log::warn!` the prepare + query error branches so the failure is
observable (matches the crate's `[dedup]`/store-open warn style).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jobs): guard prefs saves pre-load, host fallback label, distinct-count denominator

PR #756 review findings (renderer):

- TechStackPreferences + JobLocationPreferences: early-return each handler when
  useJobPreferences is still undefined (pre-load). The full-row
  useSetJobPreferences write spread `{...undefined, field}`, NULLing every other
  column — now including extraAgencyCompanies. One guard test per component.
- JobDetailPane "All sources": the no-board fallback label rendered the raw url
  while ClusterSourceChips used a shortened host. Hoisted hostOf to a shared
  components/job/host-of util; both surfaces now render the same fallback.
- JobsPage count denominator now counts distinct jobs (clusterCanonical !==
  false) instead of raw postings, so the text filter + hideAgency reduce the
  numerator against a distinct-jobs base.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

head_commit.message:
feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicate jobs with source chips and split (adr-029) (#756)

* feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicates with pair tombstones

Recompute-at-ingest cross-board job clustering per ADR-029. One pure module
(scraping/cluster) blocks by normalized company + title first-token, then joins
by cosine (cached same-space vectors) or trigram-Jaccard of normalized titles;
a user "not a duplicate" pair tombstone against any member vetoes the join.

Surfaces:
- scraping/cluster/{normalize,mod}.rs: fold/company/title normalization, agency
  flag, trigram-Jaccard, assign_clusters, new_cluster_count (+ heavy unit tests).
- vector.rs (L0): hoisted cosine, re-exported from commands::ai_provider so the
  L1 cluster module reuses it without an upward import (arch R7).
- dedup/mod.rs: DedupStore on dedup.db (pair tombstones, DataStore + Resettable).
- commands/scrape.rs::recluster_postings_cache wired after both scrape paths;
  postings::apply_cluster_annotations patches cache items by id.
- autopilot: FoundJob cluster fields; record_run clusters the merged list and
  returns the all-new-cluster count; cluster-aware minMatchScore retention.
- IPC: dedup_mark_not_duplicate (5-file flow, gen:ipc) + JobPreferences
  extraAgencyCompanies single-column setter; tauri-client + service hooks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(scraping): clamp dedup split request at the command boundary

- dedup_mark_not_duplicate: server-side clamp (CWE-770) mirroring
  clamp_agency_list — cap other_keys at 32, byte-clamp member_key + each
  other_key (~200 B), drop blank/self keys; no-op when nothing usable. Never
  trusts the renderer's Zod bounds alone. Colocated tests cover the caps.
- scraping/cluster: normalize the agency extras once per assign_clusters call
  (is_agency_with) instead of re-normalizing the full list per posting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jobs): render cross-board clusters with source chips and agency filter

Renderer side of ADR-029 (Feature A). The backend already annotates postings
and autopilot found-jobs with clusterId/clusterCanonical/clusterMembers/isAgency;
this wires the UI to group, echo, and split them.

- Posting type gains the four optional cluster fields (matching the backend JSON).
- Shared ClusterSourceChips + AgencyChip in components/job (both features import
  them without crossing feature dirs); chips open member urls via useOpenExternal.
- Jobs list hides clusterCanonical===false rows (unannotated always shown) and
  adds a hideAgency session toggle next to the sort control.
- PostingRow / PostingListItem / JobDetailPane render source chips + agency chip;
  JobDetailPane adds an "All sources" section with a per-member "Not a duplicate"
  split calling useMarkNotDuplicate.
- AutopilotCard renders one row per cluster (counts track visible clusters) plus
  chips + agency + a split action scoped by autopilotId.
- Settings: AgencyCompaniesPreferences edits extraAgencyCompanies via the
  single-column setter.
- en/de i18n for cluster/agency/settings keys, TEST_IDS, and colocated tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(scraping): add cluster acceptance fixtures for cross-board dedup

Close the ADR-029 acceptance gap: the same real-world job, parsed through two
real board parsers with two different wire shapes (germantechjobs XML feed +
Adzuna JSON), must collapse into one cluster with both source refs and the
canonical-preference winner. Fixture-driven, no network.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jobs): keep cluster chips non-interactive inside the split-view listbox

frontend-reviewer HIGH: focusable chip Buttons inside PostingListItem — a
role="option" row with tabIndex=-1 in the active-descendant listbox — broke the
APG pattern (option must have no focusable descendants), exploded tab stops, and
risked focus loss when the virtualizer unmounts a focused chip.

- ClusterSourceChips gains `interactive` (default true); when false it renders
  pure presentational badges (no Button, no click) so the option row stays a
  single tab stop. PostingListItem passes interactive={false}; PostingRow and
  JobDetailPane keep the interactive chips (the "open other source" action lives
  in the detail pane's "All sources" section).
- PostingListItem.test now renders the REAL chips and asserts the option row has
  zero focusable descendants + a chip click selects once (no double action).
- Harden SourceBadge: guard the PLATFORM_CONFIG lookup with Object.hasOwn so a
  hostile source (__proto__/constructor) falls through to the generic fallback
  instead of resolving an Object.prototype member and crashing on config.icon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(jobs): cover autopilot split action and dedup invalidation paths

testing-reviewer follow-ups (tests only):

- AutopilotCard: assert the cluster split fires markNotDuplicate with
  { memberKey, otherKeys, autopilotId } — the autopilotId is load-bearing (only
  this call site sends it, ADR-029 §h autopilot-record recompute). Forward
  data-testid through the test's Button stub so the split button is queryable.

served); repoints the generator, secret-scan list, and prettierignore; cleanup
sweep of the landing app found nothing dead. Docs mark PR1-PR3 shipped.

* docs: mark landing chain pr1-pr3 shipped in status and adr

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: restore adr addendum lost to a stale-tree copy; mark chain pr1-pr3 shipped

The previous docs commit copied file versions from a checkout that was two
commits behind origin/main, wiping the pr2 adr addendum (origin invariant,
next 16 constraint). This restores the full current docs and re-applies the
intended chain-status edits plus the stale google-fonts row fix.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: self-host social-card template fonts from public fonts dir

---------

head_commit.message:
feat: landing docs tier — docshell, mission control, agent-system route, next 16 (#742)

* feat: landing docs tier - docshell, mission control, agent-system route, next 16

Shared landing tokens + DocShell (agent-system dark hand-drawn look). /agent-system
ported to a typed-data React route; /how-it-works reskinned onto the shell (copy
verbatim); ci-dashboard deleted and replaced by /mission-control - a verdict-first
whole-repo dashboard (DORA-lite, CHAOSS starter health, benchmark sparklines,
community stats) with fine-grained-PAT sign-in and confirm-gated safe-tier write
actions (never merges). Next.js 16.2.10 (TS held at 6, documented); liveOrSnapshot
data seam ready for the PR4 snapshot plane. Security review APPROVE (frame-buster,
img-src tightened, origin invariant recorded); all ui-ux findings fixed incl.
empty-sample null-guards, AA contrast token, and reskin tint re-tokenization.
architecture-map port deferred to its own PR (stays passthrough).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: document landing coverage and ts pin protection in dependabot config

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address all pr2 review findings - self-hosted fonts, fail-closed guards, verdict semantics

Self-hosts all 6 font families site-wide (20 woff2 + fonts.css; GoogleFonts component
deleted; CSP font/style sources tightened to self) closing the origin-invariant
contradiction CodeRabbit flagged. Enforces write-action needs at the confirm choke
point, wires cacheTtlMs, serves stale cache on rate-limit 403, makes the script-src
allowlist fail-closed, treats skipped/neutral workflow conclusions as non-red with a
decisive-conclusion scan, stabilizes the confirm-dialog focus trap, and matches danger
styling on the pages dispatch. Docs: TS-pin row corrected, ADR literals -> manifest
refs, origin invariant extended to stylesheets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
